claims.text: Homeschooling allows customized pacing and curriculum
Public schools provide socialization opportunities that are hard to replicate
claims.confidence_rationale: Evidence is mixed; depends heavily on homeschool approach

claims.text: A child's educational needs should be the primary driver

evidence.text: Multiple studies show homeschooled students achieve at or above grade level
Homeschooled students report fewer peer interactions on average

perspectives.name: The Individualized Learning Advocate
perspectives.long_description: Believes each child learns differently and that rigid school structures harm many students who would thrive with personalized pacing.

perspectives.name: The Social Development Prioritizer
perspectives.short_description: Prioritizes peer interaction and collaborative skills
perspectives.long_description: Believes that social development is inseparable from education and that schools provide irreplaceable peer learning environments.

value_dimensions.name: Academic Customization
value_dimensions.description: How much weight to place on tailored instruction vs standardized curriculum

tensions.text: Individualized Learning vs Social Development advocates weigh socialization evidence differently
